title: Harness engineering: leveraging Codex in an agent-first world

sections.title: The Zero-Code Experiment
key_points: Built entire software product with 0 manually-written lines of code using Codex
Product has daily users and external alpha testers
Estimated 1/10th the development time compared to manual coding
Generated ~1 million lines of code over 5 months
Team of 3-7 engineers managing agents, averaging 3.5 PRs per engineer per day

sections.title: Redefining Engineering Roles
key_points: Engineers focus on systems, scaffolding, and leverage rather than direct coding
Primary job became enabling agents to do useful work
Work depth-first: break down goals into building blocks for agents
Humans interact primarily through prompts and review processes
Agent-to-agent review processes with Ralph Wiggum Loop methodology

sections.title: Making Applications Agent-Legible
key_points: Made UI, logs, and metrics directly readable by Codex
Implemented per-worktree app instances for isolated testing
Integrated Chrome DevTools Protocol for UI interaction
Exposed observability via LogQL and PromQL queries
Agents can work autonomously for 6+ hours on single tasks

sections.title: Repository Knowledge Management
key_points: Abandoned monolithic AGENTS.md approach due to context crowding and rot
Structured docs/ directory as system of record with progressive disclosure
AGENTS.md serves as table of contents (~100 lines)
Mechanical enforcement via linters and CI jobs
Recurring doc-gardening agent maintains documentation freshness

sections.title: Architecture and Quality Enforcement
key_points: Rigid architectural model with strict layer dependencies
Custom linters enforce invariants and taste preferences
Business domains follow fixed layer structure (Types → Config → Repo → Service → Runtime → UI)
Constraints enable speed without architectural drift
Error messages include remediation instructions for agents

sections.title: High-Throughput Development Philosophy
key_points: Minimal blocking merge gates due to high agent throughput
Short-lived pull requests with quick corrections over waiting
Agents handle entire development lifecycle end-to-end
Full autonomy from bug reproduction to merge completion
Human escalation only when judgment required

sections.title: Entropy Management and Cleanup
key_points: Agents replicate existing patterns, leading to drift over time
Replaced manual Friday cleanup (20% time) with automated processes
Golden principles encoded as mechanical rules
Background Codex tasks perform continuous refactoring
Garbage collection approach to technical debt management

summary.title: Building Production Software with Zero Human-Written Code Using AI Agents
takeaways: It's possible to build and ship real software products entirely with AI agents, achieving 10x development speed
Success requires shifting from writing code to designing environments and feedback loops for agents
Agent legibility is crucial - everything must be accessible in-repository context
Strict architectural constraints and automated enforcement enable speed without decay
High-throughput development changes merge philosophy - corrections are cheaper than waiting
actionable: Structure repository knowledge as progressive disclosure rather than monolithic documentation
Implement strict architectural constraints with custom linters for agent environments
Make application state (UI, logs, metrics) directly accessible to AI agents
Build automated cleanup processes to prevent AI-generated technical debt accumulation
Design agent-to-agent review processes to reduce human bottlenecks
Encode human taste and principles as mechanical rules rather than manual guidelines

sections.title: 零代码实验
key_points: 使用Codex构建了完整的软件产品，没有手动编写任何一行代码
产品拥有日活用户和外部alpha测试者
相比手动编码，开发时间估计缩短至1/10
5个月内生成了约100万行代码
3-7名工程师团队管理agents，平均每位工程师每天3.5个PR

sections.title: 重新定义工程师角色
key_points: 工程师专注于系统、脚手架和杠杆作用，而非直接编码
主要工作变成让agents能够完成有用的工作
深度优先工作：将目标分解为agents的构建模块
人类主要通过提示和审查流程进行交互
使用Ralph Wiggum Loop方法论的agent间审查流程

sections.title: 让应用程序对Agent可读
key_points: 让UI、日志和指标能够被Codex直接读取
实现每个工作树的应用实例以进行隔离测试
集成Chrome DevTools Protocol进行UI交互
通过LogQL和PromQL查询暴露可观测性
Agents可以在单个任务上自主工作6小时以上

sections.title: 仓库知识管理
key_points: 由于上下文拥挤和腐化，放弃了单体AGENTS.md方法
将docs/目录结构化为记录系统，采用渐进式披露
AGENTS.md作为目录（约100行）
通过linters和CI作业进行机械化执行
定期文档维护agent保持文档新鲜度

sections.title: 架构和质量执行
key_points: 严格的架构模型，具有严格的层依赖关系
自定义linters执行不变量和品味偏好
业务域遵循固定的层结构（Types → Config → Repo → Service → Runtime → UI）
约束在不产生架构漂移的情况下实现速度
错误消息包含为agents提供的修复指令

sections.title: 高吞吐量开发理念
key_points: 由于agent高吞吐量，最小化阻塞合并门控
短期pull request，快速修正而非等待
Agents处理端到端的整个开发生命周期
从bug复现到合并完成的完全自主性
仅在需要判断时才升级到人类

sections.title: 熵管理和清理
key_points: Agents复制现有模式，随时间导致漂移
用自动化流程替代手动周五清理（20%时间）
将黄金原则编码为机械规则
后台Codex任务执行持续重构
技术债务管理的垃圾收集方法

summary.title: 使用AI Agents构建零人工代码的生产软件
takeaways: 完全使用AI agents构建和发布真实软件产品是可能的，可实现10倍开发速度
成功需要从编写代码转向为agents设计环境和反馈循环
Agent可读性至关重要 - 一切都必须在仓库上下文中可访问
严格的架构约束和自动化执行在不产生衰退的情况下实现速度
高吞吐量开发改变了合并理念 - 修正比等待更便宜
actionable: 将仓库知识结构化为渐进式披露，而非单体文档
为agent环境实现带有自定义linters的严格架构约束
让应用状态（UI、日志、指标）对AI agents直接可访问
构建自动化清理流程以防止AI生成的技术债务积累
设计agent间审查流程以减少人类瓶颈
将人类品味和原则编码为机械规则，而非手动指导原则
